title: USD Single-Layer Namespace Source Contract

included: wrapping one document-model Layer as a NamespaceSource
attribute and relationship spec lookup by property path
child prim name enumeration from primChildren
primOrder and propertyOrder exposure without cross-layer merging
active, instanceable, specifier, typeName, and kind field exposure as authored layer values
diagnostics passthrough from the layer-open/parser boundary
deferred: composition of multiple opinions at a path
value interpolation or fallback value resolution

model.SingleLayerNamespaceSource: A degenerate namespace source backed by exactly one document-model Layer. Every path has zero or one local spec opinion.
model.AuthoredFieldOpinion: The authored field value stored on the layer spec, prim spec, attribute spec, or relationship spec. Missing fields remain missing until a consumer applies a fallback rule.
model.ChildNameSource: The primChildren/propertyChildren fields maintained by usd-document-model. The source does not infer missing children by scanning unrelated specs.
model.SourceMode: single_layer with composition, value_resolution, payload_loading, instancing, and schema_fallbacks all declared absent.

output.exists: whether a layer or prim spec exists at the path
output.fields: authored field map visible through target-native field values
output.childNames: primChildren token list, or empty list when missing
output.propertyNames: propertyChildren token list, or empty list when missing

output.exists: whether an attribute or relationship spec exists at the path
output.fields: authored field map visible through target-native field values

input.path: absolute prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: zero or one authored scene-object opinion for this single-layer source

laws: the single-layer source consumes document-model storage and must not maintain a second independent spec store
spec path identity comes from usd-paths; adapters may accept string paths only at the conformance boundary
field names and child/property names use the document-model token/field-token identity domain
primChildren and propertyChildren are exposed in their stored order before stage-population applies primOrder/propertyOrder rules
active=false is exposed as an authored field; pruning inactive descendants is owned by usd-stage-population
specifier fallback is not applied by this provider; stage population owns query fallback policy for missing specifier fields
composition-arc fields such as references, payload, inheritPaths, specializes, variantSetNames, and variantSelection remain inert field values

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with a canonical layer object and an ops array. The adapter must convert that JSON boundary into target-native document-model values before wrapping the source.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
